name: South Korea
summary: Day 63 Thursday. KOSPI 6,712.80 (+0.3% 1D, +22.5% 1M, +28.5% 3M); SAMSUNG Q1 RECORD PROFIT (+8X YoY, OPERATING PROFIT 57.2T WON / ~$36B; HBM4 MASS PRODUCTION FOR NVIDIA VERA RUBIN); HELIUM CASCADE FALSIFICATION CONFIRMED — DAY 6 BINARY CLOSE WITH NO HELIUM MENTION. Samsung Q1 release Apr 30: revenue 133.9T won (~$100B), operating profit 57.2T won (8x YoY); memory operating profit 53.7T won (~$33.6B); HBM business surged with Samsung becoming the world's first to mass-produce and ship sixth-generation HBM4 to Nvidia Vera Rubin platform. Q2 guidance: memory demand 'strong amid AI infrastructure expansion,' first HBM4E samples Q2. NO HELIUM MENTION in earnings release or forward guidance — binary close on Phil Kornbluth falsification window (Day 6 of 6). Combined with SK Hynix Apr 23 'diversified suppliers + sufficient inventory,' helium-cascade thesis EMPIRICALLY FALSIFIED at tier-1 chipmaker level. KOSPI +22.5% 1M is AI-cycle (memory + HBM-driven), NOT crisis-conditional. USD/KRW ₩1,485.98 (+1.0% Apr 29) — parallel EM-Asia oil-sensitive currency stress with Indonesian rupiah record low + INR ₹94.82 Day 3 of ₹94.50 breach. Mag 7 beat-and-raise capex ($630-650B 2026 AI commitment) reinforces structural Korean memory demand 2027+.

active_triggers.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
